name: tools
context: Define MCP tools — executable functions that LLMs discover and invoke to perform actions like API calls, computations, and queries
info: This sub-skill covers MCP tools — the model-controlled primitive. Tools are executable functions exposed by MCP servers that language models can discover via tools/list and invoke via tools/call. Each tool has a name, description, and JSON Schema inputSchema. Tools return content arrays (text, images, resources). The LLM decides when to call tools based on context. Python: use @mcp.tool() decorator with type hints. TypeScript: use server.tool() with Zod schemas. Tools support structured output via outputSchema, error reporting via isError flag, and annotations for trust/safety metadata.
triggers: add a tool to mcp server

sections.title: Defining tools
sections.body: Python: `@mcp.tool()` decorator with typed parameters and docstring. TypeScript: `server.tool(name, description, zodSchema, handler)`. Both auto-generate JSON Schema inputSchema from type annotations. Tools can be sync or async. Names must be unique within the server.

sections.title: Protocol messages
sections.body: `tools/list` returns array of tool definitions with name, description, inputSchema, optional outputSchema and annotations. `tools/call` takes name and arguments, returns content array with optional isError flag. `notifications/tools/list_changed` signals tool list updates. Supports pagination via cursor.

sections.title: Tool responses and content types
sections.body: Tool results contain a `content` array with typed items: text (plain text), image (base64 + mimeType), audio (base64 + mimeType), resource (embedded resource with URI), resource_link (URI reference). Multiple items can be combined. Structured output adds `structuredContent` alongside content for validated JSON responses.

sections.title: Error handling
sections.body: Protocol errors use JSON-RPC error codes: -32602 for unknown tools or invalid params, -32603 for internal errors. Tool execution errors set `isError: true` in the result with descriptive text content. Clients should handle both error types. Protocol errors indicate bugs; execution errors are expected runtime failures.

links.context: Resources provide data context alongside tools
Tools are model-controlled primitives in the architecture

related.note: Tools are the primary server primitive
Resources complement tools by providing data context
Clients are the consumers of tools

scripts.name: Add Tool to Python Server
scripts.description: Add a new tool definition to an existing FastMCP server

outputSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema for structured output

content.description: Array of content items (text, image, resource)

depends.reason: Tools are defined within an MCP server

author: dojo-community
